message:
feat(ufunc): add copy-on-write views, fine-grained memory overlap detection, and broadcast_to_view

UFuncArrayView gains several NumPy-parity capabilities:

1. Writable flag + read-only views: A new `writable` field lets views be
   marked read-only (e.g. broadcast views that alias data via zero
   strides). `as_read_only()` clones the view with writable=false,
   and `is_writable()` exposes the flag. The writable state propagates
   through slice_axis and reshape_view so derived views inherit it.

2. Copy-on-write mutation (`itemset_cow`): Before mutating, checks
   the writable flag (returning an error for read-only views) then
   detaches the shared buffer via Arc::strong_count > 1 clone, ensuring
   sibling views are never corrupted by a write.

3. Fine-grained `shares_memory` and `may_share_memory`: The old
   `shares_memory` only checked Arc pointer identity, giving false
   positives for disjoint slices of the same buffer. The new version
   computes each view's offset span (min/max byte range) for a fast
   bounding-box check, then — for arrays under 200k elements — collects
   the exact set of touched offsets and performs an intersection test.
   `may_share_memory` is the conservative (span-only) variant, matching
   NumPy's two-tier API.

4. `broadcast_to_view`: Produces a read-only view with zero strides on
   broadcast dimensions, matching np.broadcast_to semantics. The result
   is non-writable because writes through aliased zero-stride positions
   would be semantically unsound.

All four capabilities are covered by new unit tests verifying disjoint
slices, strided non-overlap, true overlap detection, broadcast read-only
enforcement, and COW buffer detachment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
